claim_text: Gross static pressure rises across the current lower-right corner endpoint pair, but the rise is hydrostatic dominated.
After hydrostatic and kinetic correction, the signed available residual is small and negative and is preserved as section-effective pressure-recovery diagnostic evidence.
Current evidence supports a section-effective recirculating pressure-residual interpretation, not an ordinary single-stream component minor-loss coefficient.
The corner has negative loss or admits a negative component K.
The July 21 result admits F6, a hidden global multiplier, or a universal component-K closure.
